auth.emails_description: Configure what emails your users receive and how they are sent

home.oriole_db_warning: This project is using Postgres with OrioleDB which is currently in preview and not suitable for production workloads. View our <link>documentation</link> for all limitations.

new_project_panel.welcome_description: Your project has been deployed on its own instance, with its own API all set up and ready to use.
new_project_panel.build_database_description: Start building your app by creating tables and inserting data. Our Table Editor makes Postgres as easy to use as a spreadsheet, but there's also our SQL Editor if you need something more.
new_project_panel.explore_products_description: Supabase provides all the backend features you need to build a product. You can use it completely, or just the features you need.
new_project_panel.authentication_description: A complete user management system that works without any additional tools.
new_project_panel.storage_description: Store, organize, and serve any file types of any size from multiple buckets.
new_project_panel.edge_functions_description: Write custom code without deploying or scaling servers, with fast deploy times and low latency.
new_project_panel.realtime_description: Listen to your PostgreSQL database in realtime via websockets.
new_project_panel.connect_description: Interact with your database through the Supabase client libraries and your API keys.

status.description_paused: This project will not accept requests until resumed

status.description_coming_up: Your project will be ready in a few minutes

compute.free_upgrade_desc: Paid plans include a free upgrade to Micro compute.

organizations.title: Your Organizations
organizations.not_found_description: The organization <1>{{slug}}</1> does not exist or you do not have permission to access to it. Contact the the owner if you believe this is a mistake.

table_editor.table_not_found: Unable to find your table with ID {{id}}
table_editor.table_not_found_description: This table doesn't exist in your database
table_editor.connection_string_missing: Unable to run query: Connection string is missing
table_editor.explain_single_statement_only: EXPLAIN only works on a single SQL statement. Please select just one query to analyze.
table_editor.debug_prompt:
Help me to debug the attached sql snippet which gives the following error: 

{{error}}
table_editor.debug_failed: Sorry, the assistant failed to debug your query! Please try again with a different one.
table_editor.placeholder: Hit {{shortcut}} to generate query or just start typing
table_editor.limit_results_description: Results are automatically limited to preserve browser performance, in particular if your query returns an exceptionally large number of rows.
table_editor.limit_results_change: You may change or remove this limit from the dropdown on the right
table_editor.name: Name
table_editor.enable_rls_description: Restrict access to your table by enabling RLS and writing Postgres policies.
table_editor.enable_realtime_description: Broadcast changes on this table to authorized subscribers
table_editor.duplicate_rows_description: This will copy all the data in the table into the new table
table_editor.rls_required_title: Policies are required to query data
table_editor.rls_required_description: You need to create an access policy before you can query data from this table. Without a policy, querying this table will return an <1>empty array</1> of results. {{isNewRecord}}
table_editor.rls_create_after_saving: You can create policies after saving this table.
table_editor.rls_anonymous_title: You are allowing anonymous access to your table
table_editor.rls_anonymous_description: {{tableName}} will be publicly writable and readable
table_editor.discard_changes_description: There are unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to close the panel? Your changes will be lost.
table_editor.import_data_summary: Your table will be created with {{rowCount}} rows and the following {{columnCount}} columns.
table_editor.no_primary_keys_description: Tables should have at least one column as the primary key to identify each row. Without a primary key, you will not be able to update or delete rows from the table.
table_editor.composite_primary_key_description: The columns that you've selected will be grouped as a primary key, and will serve as the unique identifier for the rows in your table
table_editor.column_name_tooltip: Recommended to use lowercase and use an underscore to separate words e.g. column_name
table_editor.column_default_value_tooltip: Can either be a literal or an expression. When using an expression wrap your expression in brackets, e.g. (gen_random_uuid())
table_editor.foreign_keys_error: Failed to retrieve foreign key relationships
table_editor.column_type_locked: Column type cannot be changed as it has a foreign key relation
table_editor.is_identity_description: Automatically assign a sequential unique number to the column
table_editor.define_as_array_description: Allow column to be defined as variable-length multidimensional arrays
table_editor.is_primary_key_description: A primary key indicates that a column or group of columns can be used as a unique identifier for rows in the table
table_editor.allow_nullable_description: Allow the column to assume a NULL value if no value is provided
table_editor.is_unique_description: Enforce values in the column to be unique across rows

realtime.create_realtime_experiences_description: Send your first realtime message from your database, application code or edge function
realtime.broadcast_messages_description: Send messages to a channel from your client application or database via triggers.
realtime.write_policies_description: Set up Row Level Security policies to control who can see messages within a channel
realtime.subscribe_to_channel_description: Receive realtime messages in your application by listening to a channel
realtime.channel_description: The channel you initialize with the Supabase Realtime client. Learn more in <link>our docs</link>
realtime.is_channel_private_description: If the channel is marked as private, it will use RLS policies to filter messages.
realtime.leave_channel_warning: If you leave this channel, all of the messages populated on this page will disappear
realtime.presence_description: Store and synchronize user state consistently across clients
realtime.broadcast_description: Send any data to any client subscribed to the same channel
realtime.database_changes_description: Listen for Database inserts, updates, deletes and more
realtime.enable_realtime_publications: Enable <link>realtime publications</link> for your tables to listen for database changes
realtime.learn_more_filtering: Learn more about realtime filtering in <link>our docs</link>
realtime.confirm_clear_messages_title: Previously found messages will be lost
realtime.confirm_clear_messages_description: The realtime inspector will clear currently collected messages and start listening for new messages matching the updated filters.
realtime.policies_description: Control access to your realtime channels

table_editor.read_only_tooltip: You need additional permissions to manage your project's data
table_editor.rls_description_1: You can restrict and control who can read, write and update data in this table using Row Level Security.
table_editor.rls_description_2: With RLS enabled, anonymous users will not be able to read/write data in the table.
table_editor.index_advisor_recommendation: Index Advisor recommends indexes to improve query performance on this table.
table_editor.index_advisor_enable_recommendation: Enable Index Advisor to get recommendations based on your actual query patterns.
table_editor.realtime_tooltip_enable: Click to enable realtime for this table
table_editor.realtime_tooltip_disable: Click to disable realtime for this table
table_editor.security_definer_description_1: This view is defined with the Security Definer property, giving it permissions of the view's creator (Postgres), rather than the permissions of the querying user.
table_editor.security_definer_description_2: Since this view is in the public schema, it is accessible via your project's APIs.
table_editor.secure_foreign_table_description: Foreign tables do not enforce RLS, which may allow unrestricted access. To secure them, either move foreign tables to a private schema not exposed by PostgREST, or <1>disable PostgREST access</1> entirely.
table_editor.confirm_realtime_description: Once realtime has been {{action}}, the table will {{no_longer}}broadcast any changes to authorized subscribers.
table_editor.confirm_realtime_note: You may also select which events to broadcast to subscribers on the <1>database publications</1> settings.
table_editor.confirm_rls_description: Are you sure you want to {{action}} Row Level Security for this table?

secrets.title: Edge Function Secrets
secrets.description: Manage encrypted values for your functions
secrets.local_secrets_description: Local secrets and environment variables can be loaded in either of the following two ways
secrets.env_file_method: Through an <1>.env</1> file placed at <3>supabase/functions/.env</3>, which is automatically loaded on <5>supabase start</5>
secrets.env_flag_method: Through the <1>--env-file</1> option for <3>supabase functions serve</3>, for example: <5>supabase functions serve --env-file ./path/to/.env-file</5>
secrets.secrets_runtime_injection: Secrets can also be loaded at runtime by injecting them into

functions.create_via_editor_description: Create and edit functions directly in the browser. Download to local at any time.
functions.create_via_assistant_description: Let our AI assistant help you create functions. Perfect for kickstarting a function.

local_instructions.create_new_function: Create a new edge function called <1>hello-world</1> in your project via the Supabase CLI.

local_instructions.invoke_locally_description: While serving your local Edge Functions, you can invoke it using cURL or one of the client libraries.
local_instructions.self_hosting_description: Supabase Edge Runtime consists of a web server based on the Deno runtime, capable of running Javascript, Typescript, and WASM services. You may self-host edge functions on providers like Fly.io, Digital Ocean, or AWS.

users.search_all_columns_info: This will allow you to search across user ID, email, phone number, and display name through a single input field. You will also be able to filter users by provider and sort on users across different columns.

users.index_monitor_info: You can monitor the progress in the <link>project logs</link>. If you encounter any issues, please contact Supabase support for assistance.

smtp.custom_smtp_description: Emails will be sent using your custom SMTP provider. Email rate limits can be adjusted <link>here</link>.

smtp.sender_details_description: Configure the sender information for your emails.

smtp.sender_name_description: Name displayed in the recipient's inbox.

smtp.host_description: Hostname or IP address of your SMTP server.
smtp.check_smtp_provider_description: It looks like the SMTP provider you entered is designed for sending personal rather than transactional email messages. Email deliverability may be impacted.
smtp.port_number_description: Port used by your SMTP server. Common ports include 465 and 587. Avoid using port 25 as it is often blocked by providers to curb spam.
smtp.minimum_interval_description: The minimum time in seconds between emails before another email can be sent to the same user.
smtp.password_description: Password for your SMTP server. For security reasons, this password cannot be viewed once saved.
smtp.rate_limit_increased: Rate limit for sending emails will be increased to 30 and <link>can be adjusted</link> after enabling custom SMTP
smtp.rate_limit_reduced: Rate limit for sending emails will be reduced to 2 after disabling custom SMTP

validation.valid_port_min: Must be a valid port number more than 0
validation.valid_port_max: Must be a valid port number no more than 65535
validation.rate_limit_max: Must not be more than 32,767 an hour

site_url.title: Site URL
site_url.description: Configure the default redirect URL used when a redirect URL is not specified or doesn't match one from the allow list. This value is also exposed as a template variable in the email templates section. Wildcards cannot be used here.

redirect_urls.title: Redirect URLs
redirect_urls.description: URLs that auth providers are permitted to redirect to post authentication. Wildcards are allowed, for example, https://*.domain.com

infrastructure.read_replicas_moved_desc: Read replicas is now managed under Replication in the Database section.
infrastructure.service_versions_desc: Service versions and upgrade eligibility for your provisioned instance.
infrastructure.service_versions_paused_title: Service versions cannot be retrieved while project is paused
infrastructure.service_versions_paused_desc: Restoring the project will update Postgres to the newest version
infrastructure.release_channel_tooltip: This project uses a {{channel}} database version release
infrastructure.latest_tooltip: Project is on the latest version of Postgres that Supabase supports
infrastructure.api_load_balancer_desc: Distributes incoming API requests across {{count}} databases
infrastructure.drop_replica_permission_error: You need additional permissions to drop replicas
infrastructure.replica_init_failed_tooltip: Replica failed to initialize. Please drop this replica and spin up a new one.
infrastructure.postgres_upgrade_available: A newer version of Postgres is available
infrastructure.upgrade_remove_replicas_desc: You will need to remove all read replicas prior to upgrading your Postgres version to the latest available ({{latestPgVersion}}).
infrastructure.upgrade_validation_intro: The following issues must be resolved before upgrading.

upgrade_validation.objects_depending_on_pg_cron: Remove objects that depend on pg_cron
upgrade_validation.indexes_referencing_ll_to_earth: Drop or recreate the index on table {{table}} that references ll_to_earth()
upgrade_validation.function_using_obsolete_lang: Update the function to use a supported language instead of {{lang}}
upgrade_validation.unsupported_fdw_handler: Update or remove the FDW using the obsolete handler {{handler}}
upgrade_validation.unlogged_table_with_persistent_sequence: Convert the sequence {{sequence}} to unlogged or convert the table to logged
upgrade_validation.user_defined_objects_in_internal_schemas: Move the {{objType}} to your own schema

infrastructure.restart_replica_desc: Your replica will be offline for a few minutes while it is being restarted. Before restarting the replica, consider:
infrastructure.restart_replica_notice_1: Network traffic from this region may slow down while the replica is restarting, especially if you have no other replicas in this region
infrastructure.restart_replica_confirm_question: Are you sure you want to restart this replica now?
infrastructure.drop_replica_alert_desc: You may still deploy a new replica in this region thereafter
infrastructure.drop_replica_notice_1: Network traffic from this region may slow down, especially if you have no other replicas in this region
infrastructure.no_read_replicas: Your project has no read replicas
infrastructure.confirm_drop_all_replicas_title: Confirm to drop all read replicas?
infrastructure.drop_all_replicas_alert_desc: You may still deploy new replicas in this region thereafter
infrastructure.drop_all_replicas_notice_1: Network traffic from this region may slow down
infrastructure.deploy_replica_permission_error: You need additional permissions to deploy replicas
infrastructure.read_replicas_not_supported: Read replicas are not supported with OrioleDB
infrastructure.resolve_invoices_first: Please resolve all outstanding invoices first before deploying a new read replica
infrastructure.read_replicas_aws_only_title: Read replicas are only supported for projects provisioned via AWS
infrastructure.read_replicas_aws_only_desc: Projects provisioned by other cloud providers currently will not be able to use read replicas
infrastructure.read_replicas_aws_revamped_title: Read replicas are not supported for AWS (Revamped) projects
infrastructure.read_replicas_pg_version_title: Read replicas can only be deployed with projects on Postgres version 15 and above
infrastructure.contact_support_for_replicas: If you'd like to use read replicas, please contact us via support
infrastructure.small_compute_required_title: Project required to at least be on a Small compute
infrastructure.small_compute_required_desc: This is to ensure that read replicas can keep up with the primary databases' activities.
infrastructure.enabling_physical_backups: Physical backups are currently being enabled
infrastructure.physical_backups_required: Physical backups are required to deploy replicas
infrastructure.physical_backups_desc: Physical backups are used under the hood to spin up read replicas for your project.
infrastructure.check_back_later: This warning will go away once physical backups have been enabled - check back in a few minutes!
infrastructure.enable_physical_backups_desc: Enabling physical backups will take a few minutes, after which you will be able to deploy read replicas.
infrastructure.start_deploying_after: You may start deploying read replicas thereafter once this is completed.
infrastructure.spend_cap_disabled_title: Spend cap needs to be disabled to deploy replicas
infrastructure.spend_cap_disabled_desc: Launching a replica incurs additional disk size that will exceed the plan's quota. Disable the spend cap first to allow overages before launching a replica.
infrastructure.max_replicas_title: You can only deploy up to {{count}} read replicas at once
infrastructure.max_replicas_desc: If you'd like to spin up another read replica, please drop an existing replica first.
infrastructure.upgrade_compute_for_more: Alternatively, you may deploy up to {{max}} replicas if your project is on an XL compute or higher.
infrastructure.select_region_label: Select a region to deploy your read replica in
infrastructure.replica_cost_summary: New replica will cost an additional {{cost}}/month
infrastructure.replica_cost_desc: Read replicas will match the compute size of your primary database and will include 25% more disk size than the primary database to accommodate WAL files.
infrastructure.cost_breakdown: The additional cost for the replica breaks down to:
infrastructure.old_disk_management_desc: Read replicas will be on the same compute size as your primary database. Deploying a read replica on the {{size}} size incurs additional {{cost}}.
infrastructure.read_more_billing: Read more about <link>billing</link> for read replicas.

connection_pooling.title: Connection pooling
connection_pooling.dedicated_pooler_ipv4_incompatible_title: Dedicated Pooler is not IPv4 compatible
connection_pooling.dedicated_pooler_ipv4_incompatible_desc: If your network only supports IPv4, consider purchasing the <link>IPv4 add-on</link>.
connection_pooling.update_permission_error: You need additional permissions to update connection pooling settings
connection_pooling.failed_retrieve_config: Failed to retrieve connection pooler configuration
connection_pooling.start_new_project: Please start a new project to enable this feature
connection_pooling.config_shared: Configuration is shared across all connection poolers.
connection_pooling.connection_pool_size_desc: The maximum number of connections made to the underlying Postgres cluster, per user+db combination. Pool size has a default of {{size}} based on your compute size of {{computeSize}}.
connection_pooling.pool_size_warning_title: Pool size is greater than 80% of the max connections ({{max}}) on your database
connection_pooling.pool_size_warning_desc: This may result in instability and unreliability with your database connections.
connection_pooling.max_client_connections_desc: The maximum number of concurrent client connections allowed. This value is fixed at {{count}} based on your compute size of {{computeSize}} and cannot be changed.

network_restrictions.title: Network restrictions
network_restrictions.update_permission_error: You need additional permissions to update network restrictions
network_restrictions.apply_error_title: Your network restrictions were not applied correctly
network_restrictions.apply_error_desc: Please try to add your network restrictions again
network_restrictions.access_allowed_title: Your database can be accessed by all IP addresses
network_restrictions.access_allowed_desc: You may start limiting access to your database by adding a network restriction.
network_restrictions.access_disallowed_title: Your database cannot be accessed externally
network_restrictions.access_disallowed_desc: All external IP addresses have been disallowed from accessing your project's database.
network_restrictions.access_restricted_desc_1: Only the following IP addresses have access to your database.
network_restrictions.access_restricted_desc_2: You may remove all of them to allow all IP addresses to have access to your database.
network_restrictions.restrictions_note: Note: Restrictions only apply to your database, and not to Supabase services

api_settings.unavailable_not_active: API settings are unavailable as the project is not active
api_settings.exposed_schemas_desc: The schemas to expose in your API. Tables, views and stored procedures in these schemas will get API endpoints.
api_settings.public_schema_not_exposed: The public schema for this project is not exposed
api_settings.public_schema_not_exposed_desc_1: You will not be able to query tables and views in the
api_settings.public_schema_not_exposed_desc_2: schema via supabase-js or HTTP clients.
api_settings.graphql_exposed_desc_2: schema are still exposed over our GraphQL endpoints.
api_settings.extra_search_path_desc: Extra schemas to add to the search path of every request.
api_settings.max_rows_desc: The maximum number of rows returned from a view, table, or stored procedure. Limits payload size for accidental or malicious requests.
api_settings.pool_size_desc: Number of maximum connections to keep open in the Data API server's database pool. Unset to let it be configured automatically based on compute size.
api_settings.update_permission_error: You need additional permissions to update your project's API settings
api_settings.harden_data_api_desc: Expose a custom schema instead of the public schema

harden_api.title: Switch the default API schema
harden_api.subtitle: Expose a custom schema instead of the <1>public</1> schema
harden_api.description: By default, the <1>public</1> schema is used to generate API routes. In some cases, it's better to use a custom schema. This is important if you use tools that generate tables in the <3>public</3> schema to <5>prevent accidental exposure of data</5>.
harden_api.step_1: 1. Create a custom <1>api</1> schema and expose it
harden_api.step_1_description: Click the button below to create a new schema named <1>api</1> and grant the <3>anon</3> and <5>authenticated</5> roles usage privileges on this schema. This schema will thereafter also be exposed to the Data API.
harden_api.how_schema_created_desc: The following query will be run to create the <1>api</1> schema , as well as to grant the necessary privileges to the respective roles
harden_api.create_expose_button: Create and expose schema to Data API
harden_api.schema_already_exposed: Schema has already been created and exposed
harden_api.permissions_note: Under these new settings, the <1>anon</1> and <3>authenticated</3> roles can execute functions defined in the <5>api</5> schema, but they have no automatic permissions on any tables. On a table-by-table basis, you can grant them permissions by running the following command:
harden_api.step_2: 2. Remove the <1>public</1> schema from the exposed schemas
harden_api.warning_title: Ensure that your app is no longer using the <1>public</1> schema
harden_api.warning_desc: The <1>public</1> schema will not be accessible via the API once its not exposed. You should be using the <3>api</3> schema instead.
harden_api.remove_public_desc: Click the button below to remove the <1>public</1> schema from both Exposed schemas and Extra search path in your API configuration.
harden_api.remove_public_button: Remove public schema from exposed schemas
harden_api.success_create_expose: Successfully created api schema and exposed via Data API
harden_api.success_remove_public: Success removed public schema from exposed schemas

password.title: Database password
password.reset_description: The database password isn’t viewable after creation. Resetting it will break any existing connections.
password.permission_error: You need additional permissions to reset the database password
password.project_not_active: Unable to reset database password as project is not active

connect.project_inactive: Project is currently not active and cannot be connected
connect.title: Connect to your project
connect.description: Get the connection strings and environment variables for your app.
connect.choose_how_to_use: Choose how you want to use Supabase
connect.learn: Learn how to connect to your Postgres databases.

methods.direct_description: Ideal for applications with persistent and long-lived connections, such as those running on virtual machines or long-standing containers.
methods.transaction_description: Ideal for stateless applications like serverless functions where each interaction with Postgres is brief and isolated.
methods.session_description: Only recommended as an alternative to Direct Connection, when connecting via an IPv4 network.

steps.add_configuration_package: Add the configuration package to read the settings

connect.shared_pooler_note: Only recommended when your network does not support IPv6. Added latency compared to dedicated pooler.
connect.sql_alchemy_note: Please use postgresql:// instead of postgres:// as your dialect when connecting via SQLAlchemy.
connect.reset_password_note: You may reset your database password in your project's <1>Database Settings</1>
connect.ipv4_addon_text: Connections are IPv4 proxied with IPv4 add-on
connect.pgbouncer_enabled_no_ipv4_text: Purchase IPv4 add-on or use Shared Pooler if on a IPv4 network
connect.use_session_pooler_or_purchase_ipv4: Use Session Pooler if on a IPv4 network or purchase IPv4 add-on
connect.session_pooler_ipv4_proxied_free: Session pooler connections are IPv4 proxied for free
connect.transaction_pooler_ipv4_proxied_free: Transaction pooler connections are IPv4 proxied for free.
connect.transaction_pooler_unavailable: Transaction pooler is unavailable as pool mode is set to Session
connect.update_pool_mode_transaction: If you'd like to use transaction mode, update your pool mode to Transaction for the connection pooler in your project's Database Settings.
connect.only_use_on_ipv4_network: Only use on a IPv4 network
connect.use_direct_connection_ipv6: Use Direct Connection if connecting via an IPv6 network.
connect.platforms_ipv4_only_description: A few major platforms are IPv4-only and may not work with a Direct Connection:
connect.purchase_ipv4_support: If you wish to use a Direct Connection with these, please purchase <1>IPv4 support</1>.
connect.use_session_or_transaction_pooler: You may also use the Session Pooler or Transaction Pooler if you are on a IPv4 network.

errors.api_error: Error: API error happened while trying to communicate with the server.

errors.no_permission_description: Contact your organization owner or administrator for assistance.

database.indexes_description: Improve query performance against your database
database.policies_description: Restrict and control who can read, write and update data
database.settings_description: Manage your database connection and settings

advisor.title: Index Advisor
advisor.enable_description: Recommends indexes to improve query performance.
advisor.enable_dialog_description: This will enable the <1>index_advisor</1> and <3>hypopg</3> Postgres extensions so Index Advisor can analyse queries and suggest performance-improving indexes.
advisor.indexes_in_use_description: This query is using the following index{{plural}}:
advisor.no_indexes_used: No indexes are involved in this query
advisor.no_indexes_used_description: Indexes may not necessarily be used if they incur a higher cost when executing the query
advisor.recommendations_not_available_descriptio: Index advisor could not analyze this query. This can happen if the query references tables, functions, or extensions that no longer exist or were deleted.
advisor.query_optimized_description: Recommendations for indexes will show here
advisor.recommendation_alert_description: You can improve this query's performance by <1>{{improvement}}%</1> by adding the following suggested {{indexes}}
advisor.recommendation_note: This recommendation serves to prevent your queries from slowing down as your application grows, and hence the index may not be used immediately after it's created (e.g If your table is still small at this time).
advisor.total_cost_description: An estimate of how long it will take to return all the rows (Includes start up cost)
advisor.startup_cost_description: An estimate of how long it will take to fetch the first row
advisor.apply_index_description: This will run the SQL that is shown above
advisor.faq_units_content: Costs are in an arbitrary unit, and do not represent a unit of time. The units are anchored (by default) to a single sequential page read costing 1.0 units. They do, however, serve as a predictor of higher execution times.
advisor.faq_prioritize_title: How should I prioritize start up and total cost?
advisor.faq_prioritize_content_1: This depends on the expected size of the result set from the query.
advisor.faq_prioritize_content_2: For queries that return a small number or rows, the startup cost is more critical and minimizing startup cost can lead to faster response times, especially in interactive applications.
advisor.faq_prioritize_content_3: For queries that return a large number of rows, the total cost becomes more important, and optimizing it will help in efficiently using resources and reducing overall query execution time.
advisor.failed_to_load_data: Failed to load query performance data
advisor.failed_to_load_hit_rate: Failed to load cache hit rate data
advisor.reset_report_desc: Consider resetting the analysis after optimizing any queries
advisor.how_report_generated_desc: This report uses the pg_stat_statements table, and pg_stat_statements extension. <1>Learn more here</1>.
advisor.inspect_db: Inspect your database for potential issues
advisor.inspect_db_desc: The Supabase CLI comes with a range of tools to help inspect your Postgres instances for potential issues. <1>Learn more here</1>.
advisor.reset_analysis_desc: This will reset the pg_stat_statements table in the extensions schema on your <1>{{database}}</1>, which is used to calculate query performance. This data will repopulate immediately after.

metrics.slow_queries_tooltip: Slow queries are those with total execution time (execution time + planning time) greater than 1000ms.
metrics.cache_hit_rate_tooltip: Percentage of data read from cache vs disk. Higher is better - it means faster queries and less database load.
metrics.avg_rows_per_call_tooltip: Average number of rows returned per query execution. Helps identify queries that return too much or too little data.

banner.enable_index_advisor_desc: Recommends indexes to improve query performance.

advisor.optimization_description: Adding an index will help this query execute faster
advisor.no_queries_criteria: There are no actively running queries that match the criteria
advisor.failed_load_data: Failed to load query performance data
advisor.upgrade_postgres_description: Upgrade to the latest version of Postgres to get recommendations on indexes for your queries
advisor.extensions_description: These extensions can help in recommending database indexes to reduce the costs of your query.

ai_assistant.openai_api_key_description: To use the AI Assistant, you need to set an OpenAI API key in your [project settings](/project/_/settings/auth).
ai_assistant.ask_question_or_make_change: Ask a question or make a change...

not_found.could_not_find_page: We couldn't find the page that you're looking for!

editor.performance_warning: You may try to render the entire value, but your browser may run into performance issues

support.title: Supabase support
support.assistant_description: Ask our AI assistant to help you with your support issue.
support.assistant_greeting: Hi! I'm your AI assistant. How can I help you today?
support.assistant_capabilities: I can help you with database queries, API endpoints, or any other technical questions you might have.
support.email_directly: Please email us directly. Include your project ID and as much information as possible.
support.message_placeholder: Describe the issue you’re facing, along with any relevant information. Please be as detailed and specific as possible.
support.error_reference: The error that you ran into will be included in your message for reference
support.what_are_you_having_issues_with: What are you having issues with?
support.severity_note_title: We do our best to respond to everyone as quickly as possible
support.severity_note_desc: Prioritization will be based on production status. We ask that you reserve High and Urgent severity for production-impacting issues only.
support.plan_expectations_title: Expected response times are based on your organization’s plan
support.plan_expectations_free: Support on the Free plan is provided through the community and by the team on a best-effort basis. For a guaranteed response time, we recommend upgrading to the Pro plan. Enhanced support SLAs are available on the Enterprise plan.
support.plan_expectations_pro: The Pro plan includes email support. In most cases, you can expect a response within 1 business day for all severities. For prioritized ticketing on all issues and prioritized escalation to product engineering, we recommend upgrading to the Team plan. Enhanced support SLAs are available on the Enterprise plan.
support.plan_expectations_team: The Team plan includes email support with prioritized ticketing and escalation to product engineering. Low, normal, and high-severity tickets are typically handled within 1 business day. Urgent issues are handled within 1 day, 365 days a year. Enhanced support SLAs are available on the Enterprise plan.

categories.api_client_desc: Issues with your project's API and client libraries
categories.database_unresponsive_desc: Issues with connecting to your database
categories.performance_desc: Reporting of performance issues is only available on the Pro Plan
categories.abuse_desc: Report abuse of a Supabase project or Supabase brand
categories.login_issues_desc: Issues with logging in and MFA
categories.sales_desc: Questions about pricing, paid plans and Enterprise plans
categories.billing_desc: Issues with credit card charges | invoices | overcharging
categories.refund_desc: Formal enquiry form for requesting refunds
categories.plan_upgrade_desc: Enquire a plan upgrade for your organization

suggestions.check_logs_title: Have you checked your project's logs?
suggestions.check_logs_desc: Logs can help you identify errors that you might be running into when using your project's API or client libraries. View logs for each product <1>here</1>.
suggestions.check_infra_title: Have you checked your project's infrastructure activity?
suggestions.check_infra_desc: High memory or low disk IO bandwidth may be slowing down your database. Verify by checking the infrastructure activity of your project <1>here</1>.
suggestions.check_perf_title: Have you checked the Query Performance Advisor?
suggestions.check_perf_desc: Identify slow running queries and get actionable insights on how to optimize them with the Query Performance Advisor <1>here</1>.

support.attachments_login_required: Uploads are only supported when logged in. Please reply to the acknowledgement email you will receive with any screenshots you'd like to upload.
support.attachments_description: Optionally upload up to {{max}} relevant images or <1>HAR files</1>
support.attachment_too_many: Only up to {{max}} attachments are allowed
support.dashboard_logs_description: Share sanitized logs of recent dashboard actions to help reproduce the issue.
support.support_access_allow: Allow support access to your project
support.support_access_description: Human support and AI diagnostic access.
support.support_access_details_1: By enabling this, you grant permission for our support team to access your project temporarily and, if applicable, to use AI tools to assist in diagnosing and resolving issues. This access may involve analyzing database configurations, query performance, and other relevant data to expedite troubleshooting and enhance support accuracy.
support.support_access_details_2: We are committed to maintaining strict data privacy and security standards in all support activities. <1>Privacy Policy</1>
support.contact_email_info: We will contact you at <1>{{email}}</1>. Please ensure emails from supabase.com are allowed.
support.subject: Subject
support.subject_placeholder: Summary of the problem you have
support.check_github_discussions: Check our <1>GitHub discussions</1> for a quick answer

common.replica_status_unhealthy: Replica unable to accept requests as its {{status}}. [View infrastructure settings]({{link}}) for more information.

schema_graph.auto_layout: Automatically arrange the layout of all nodes
